Hey JB, you're talking to your daily motivation slash skills training. Now you know why I say that, right? From yesterday's training. Here's the deal guys. It's Friday. It's our last day of BLEEPS week, right? I'm gonna have some more stuff coming this week and some lighter stuff, right? But I mean, this is the time where you have to really take stock in yourself. You gotta ask yourself this question, right? You know, what type of life do I wanna live? Do I wanna really have an extraordinary life? Do I wanna be an example to my children? Do I wanna support those around me, not just financially but emotionally? Do I wanna be a pillar of my community, a light that just lifts everyone else up around me? Or do I wanna be just like an average slug that moves through the world, doesn't create, doesn't give, just gets by? I don't know about you but that's like death to me. The thought of living that life is death. When I was growing up, we had this belief that was circulating around the school systems and our parents would tell us this. And we heard it on the news in the movies. It was reinforced in pop culture through TV series and movies that the real route to success and happiness in life was to go get a job at a very big company, stayed there for 25 years or so, retire, get your gold watch or whatever you get and with your pension and between that and social security that you could live a nice retirement. That turned out to be a pack of lies. The pension funds went bankrupt, they were devalued with inflation and it turned out that the social security and your pension combined was basically barreling up to if your diaper is while you were in a nursing home. It doesn't work. It was a lie. There is no golden parachute that one gets from living that life. You know what you end up getting when you take that pack? You get a golden shower. The world pisses on you. Okay? And you end up broke, no money, it disempowered, relying on others, waiting for your next hand out from the government. Is that what you want in life? Honestly, you have the ability to make the world the way you want it, to shape your life and to shape your destiny. That's your choice. And it starts with getting active in educating yourself both on the inner game of success and the outer game, the outer world skills. Do not let your life pass your by. You'll live by bad past this week, right? He was a great man. Okay? And thankfully because of me and he's the first one that would say this, thankfully that I had cracked the code for making a lot of money and I was able to retire my parents. Just so you know, I actually did retire my parents. All right? So that is an awesome thing, but there's so much not that whole thing about you're gonna retire your parents. You know, who is that guy Sam Ovens gonna retire? I don't know, a bunch of nonsense. Okay? I mean, these people, really. I mean, honestly, I did retire my parents and it feels amazing. The point is, is that, you know, you really have a chance right now to take a deep breath and just say, you know what? All right, what kind of life do I want to live? You're not gonna get a do over. Maybe you believe in multiple lives. I don't know. Maybe you do, you know? But I don't think you come back remembering your old wife, so what does it really matter? You know, they always say, this is my fifth wife. Okay, great. Tell me about the other four. Tell me. If you don't remember them, does it really matter in terms of how you're, you know, today? God, I'll go out with a bang. I mean, live your life to the fullest. Don't, you know, drag yourself out of bed. Stop out of bed in the morning. Embrace life. Go after it. Attack it. And the only way to do that is to skill up. And it starts with learning how to close. Because if you can't close, then you can't even begin to shape the world the way you want it. That's what closing's all about. It's about shaping the world the way you want it. Getting people who don't see things your way to say things your way and to pay you. As they're saying, yeah, you were right. I'll buy. That's what it's really about. It's the beginning. It's the essence. Influence and persuasion is the essence of living and empowering life. And anyone who tells you differently is full of s***. They just are. You must know how to get your point across to other people. It doesn't have to be a traditional sales setting. You could be a mom trying to get your kids to believe in a certain set of beliefs, even to make their bed, do their homework. You could be a teacher trying to sell your students on the value of education. You could be a lawyer trying to influence a jury on the innocence of your client. You could be a pastor trying to influence your congregation, a politician trying to influence your constituency. It goes on and on. Tell me someone who's living a first-class life. It doesn't have the ability to influence and persuade. Tell me. I'll give you a couple. There are some. Steve Wozniak. He's a brilliant mechanic, mechanical engineer. Are you Steve Wozniak? He's the one of the worst communicators I've ever met. But wow, what a talent that guy has. He don't need to. So if you're Steve Wozniak, good for you. Good on you. I guess you could just find the jobs. But without the jobs, he ain't going anywhere. But he's a brilliant man, and I respect him tremendously. But he would be without Steve Jobs, you know what he'd be? Working as a low-level guy in Hewlett Packard. He's a high-level guy who's just so brilliant and talented. But you know what I'm talking about. Are you Rembrandt? Do you have to say are you Michael Jordan? I don't know. I guess, yeah, there are some people. There are. I guess there are some people who can, you know, and even Michael Jordan, though, his personality, all right, makes him such a great brand. There are a handful of people in the world that can get by on sheer physical ability or beauty. He looks like a supermodel. You a supermodel? Or are you like me? I'm an average person with an incredible skill to persuade. That one skill, and I'm a smart guy as well, right? I got a lot of brainpower behind that, which is part of my ability just to close. But that's all learnable. It's learnable. You can't learn to be Roger Federer. You can't. I tried. You can't. You're not going to learn become Steve Wozniak or Michael Jordan, but you can learn to be a world-class closer. You can learn to be a first-class marketer. You can learn to be a businessman who gets rich on whatever it is you want, knows the rules of success. Your life is in your hands. So I got to just tell you, unless you got some specialized skill that you were born with in your life, one in a million, get to work. Get to work. You don't wait until your 30s like some people say. Just disagree with that. I disagree. Don't wait that long because when we taste something, you get tireder and tireder the older you get. You want to be in your 70s and your 60s still struggling. God, I've lived four lives already. I'm in my 50s. Get rich now. Money's not the be old end. I'll admit that. But I'll tell you what. I've been rich and I've been really happy and I've been rich and I've been really unhappy. I've been poor and I've been really unhappy, but I have never been poor and happy. Never. I've never been poor and happy. I promise you and I'll tell you what, I bet you're the same. All right? And if you're not, you're not being real. Money matters. It's not going to make you happy, but a lack of it will make you miserable and will cause stress in your life, early death, the inability to help those you love, to empower the community around you and just to live a great life. You deserve to live a great life. So skill up. Invest in yourself. Take action. Become strong. It starts with what you know. The skills that you can possess and learn, internalize and then use those skills in the real world. Bottom line. I love you all. Have a great weekend. You'll hear from me tomorrow again.
